senses.definition: Behaviours or attitudes that are exaggerated or not sincere, used to impress or influence other people.
examples: The debate was full of political posturings rather than real discussion.
She was tired of his dramatic posturings on social media.
Critics dismissed their threats as empty posturings.

senses.definition: To behave or speak in a way that is not natural or sincere in order to impress or influence other people.
examples: The candidate tended to posturise instead of answering the questions directly.
Commentators accused both sides of posturising for the cameras.
He prefers honest discussion and refuses to posturise in interviews.

senses.definition: Behaved or spoke in an insincere, exaggerated way to impress or influence others.
examples: Instead of discussing the issue, they just posturised and traded insults.
He posturised during the meeting to gain attention from senior managers.
The panel posturised about values without offering solutions.

senses.definition: Behaves or speaks in an exaggerated, insincere way to impress or affect others.
examples: He often posturises when journalists are present.
The party posturises about reform but rarely acts.
She posturises on social justice issues without changing her behaviour.

senses.definition: Behaving or speaking in a way that is done mainly to impress others, not because it is truly felt.
examples: There was a lot of empty posturising in the negotiations.
Instead of posturising, they need to listen to each other.
The interview turned into politicians posturising for the audience.

senses.definition: A person who often adopts exaggerated or insincere attitudes in order to impress others.
examples: To his critics, he is more of a posturist than a serious leader.
The article described the celebrity as a skilled posturist.
She tries to be genuine and not a political posturist.

senses.definition: People who frequently adopt exaggerated or insincere attitudes to impress others.
examples: The debate was dominated by posturists on both sides.
Online spaces sometimes reward loud posturists rather than thoughtful voices.
Voters are increasingly impatient with political posturists.

senses.definition: To behave or speak in an exaggerated, insincere way to impress or influence others.
examples: The spokesperson tended to posturize instead of answering directly.
They accused the government of posturizing on human rights.
He prefers to speak plainly rather than posturize.

senses.definition: Behaved or spoke in an exaggerated, insincere way to impress or influence others.
examples: They posturized throughout the hearing instead of listening.
She posturized on stage to project a tough image.
The minister posturized for the cameras after the meeting.

senses.definition: Behaves or speaks in a way that is more for show than from real feeling, in order to impress others.
examples: He often posturizes when reporters are in the room.
The party posturizes about fairness but supports the old system.
She posturizes on environmental issues without changing her habits.

senses.definition: Behaving or speaking in an exaggerated, insincere way mainly to impress or influence others.
examples: There was a lot of posturizing during the negotiations.
Instead of posturizing, they should focus on solutions.
The discussion turned into politicians posturizing for the audience.

senses.definition: Happening or located behind or after the uterus.
examples: The scan showed a postuterine mass that needed further tests.
Postuterine adhesions can cause pain and fertility problems.
The surgeon noted postuterine changes after the procedure.

senses.definition: Happening after a vaccination.
examples: Postvaccinal reactions such as fever are usually mild.
The study followed patients for postvaccinal side effects.
Postvaccinal immunity can last for many years.

senses.definition: Relating to the period or events after a vaccine has been given.
examples: Postvaccination monitoring is important to track safety.
They recorded postvaccination symptoms in all participants.
Postvaccination guidelines explain what to expect at home.

senses.definition: Occurring after a vagotomy, a surgical cutting of the vagus nerve to the stomach.
examples: Delayed stomach emptying is a known postvagotomy complication.
The team studied postvagotomy gastric function in these patients.
Postvagotomy symptoms may require diet changes.

senses.definition: Happening after an infection with varicella (chickenpox).
examples: Some children develop postvaricellar skin scarring.
The study described rare postvaricellar complications in adults.
Postvaricellar pain can last for weeks.

senses.definition: Happening after a case of varioloid, a mild form of smallpox.
examples: Researchers described postvarioloid skin changes in early reports.
Postvarioloid immunity was discussed in older medical texts.
Historic papers mention postvarioloid complications.

senses.definition: Occurring after a vasectomy, a surgical procedure for male sterilization.
examples: Postvasectomy pain is usually mild and temporary.
The clinic offers guidance on postvasectomy care.
They studied long-term postvasectomy complications.

senses.definition: Pronounced with the back of the tongue behind the soft palate in the mouth.
examples: The language has several postvelar consonants.
Postvelar sounds can be difficult for learners to hear.
She studied postvelar articulation in Caucasian languages.

senses.definition: Occurring after a venereal (sexually transmitted) disease.
examples: The report examined postvenereal complications in untreated patients.
Postvenereal symptoms can affect several organs.
Older textbooks sometimes refer to postvenereal conditions.

senses.definition: Situated or occurring behind or after a vein.
examples: The postvenous structures were difficult to see on the scan.
Postvenous bleeding was noted after the procedure.
They monitored postvenous changes in blood flow.

senses.definition: Located behind or past the ventral (front or belly) side of a body or organ.
examples: The fish has distinctive postventral markings.
Postventral muscles support this part of the body.
The study describes a postventral fin in this species.

senses.definition: Coming after the verb in a sentence.
examples: In this language, objects are usually in postverbal position.
The study focuses on postverbal subjects in Spanish.
Learners sometimes struggle with postverbal word order.

senses.definition: To turn or tilt something backward, especially the uterus.
examples: The procedure can accidentally postverta the uterus.
If the uterus postvertas, it may cause discomfort.
The doctor explained how the organ might postverta over time.

senses.definition: Located behind the vertebrae or vertebral column.
examples: The scan showed a postvertebral mass.
Postvertebral tissues were affected by the injury.
Surgeons must be careful around postvertebral structures.

senses.definition: Situated behind the urinary bladder.
examples: The MRI revealed a postvesical lesion.
Postvesical space can be involved in some pelvic infections.
They observed fluid in the postvesical area.

senses.definition: To place or mention something later in a legal or formal document.
examples: The details are postvided in section three of the contract.
The clause is postvided and does not appear in the opening section.
Conditions may be postvided at the end of the agreement.

senses.definition: Happening after a viral infection, often describing lasting symptoms.
examples: She experienced postviral fatigue for several months.
Postviral coughs can be slow to disappear.
Doctors are studying postviral conditions carefully.

senses.definition: Coming immediately after a vowel sound.
examples: In some accents, postvocalic r is not pronounced.
The study looked at postvocalic consonants in English.
Teachers often highlight postvocalic positions when teaching phonics.

senses.definition: In a way that occurs immediately after a vowel sound.
examples: In this dialect, /r/ is not pronounced postvocalically.
The consonant is softened when it appears postvocalically.
Students learned how the sound changes postvocalically.

senses.definition: Relating to the period after a war, especially after a major war.
examples: They grew up in the postwar years.
The country rebuilt its economy in the postwar period.
Postwar literature often dealt with trauma and loss.

senses.definition: Towards a post or pole, especially on a ship or in an enclosed area.
examples: The sailor moved postward along the deck.
He stepped postward to secure the rope.
They worked their way postward in the narrow space.

senses.definition: Relating to the period after a baby or young animal has stopped breastfeeding or drinking milk from its mother.
examples: The study followed children’s growth in the postweaning period.
Postweaning nutrition is crucial for healthy development.
Some animals face higher risks of disease in the postweaning stage.

senses.definition: In the direction of the post or posts, especially in fencing or construction.
examples: The fence was adjusted postwise for better alignment.
They moved postwise along the row to check each joint.
The barrier shifted slightly postwise during the storm.

senses.definition: A woman whose job is to deliver letters and parcels.
examples: The postwoman arrives at our street every morning.
We gave the friendly postwoman a thank-you card.
The postwoman handed him a large package.

senses.definition: Women whose job is to deliver letters and parcels.
examples: The city employs many postwomen and postmen.
We saw two postwomen chatting at the corner.
Postwomen often walk long distances each day.

senses.definition: Happening after a workshop or training session.
examples: They sent a postworkshop survey to all participants.
Postworkshop support is available by email.
We held a postworkshop meeting to plan next steps.

senses.definition: Located behind or below the xiphoid process, the small lower part of the breastbone.
examples: The surgeon made a postxiphoid incision.
Pain in the postxiphoid area should be assessed carefully.
A small mass was detected in the postxiphoid region.

senses.definition: Located behind or below the xiphoid process; a spelling variant of postxiphoid.
examples: A postxyphoid incision was used to access the area.
The report mentions a postxyphoid mass near the diaphragm.
Postxyphoid pain can signal different conditions.

senses.definition: An area of ground around or behind a post or group of posts, especially in a farm or work site.
examples: Tools were stored in the small postyard behind the barn.
They cleared weeds from the postyard after the storm.
The fence runs along the edge of the postyard.

senses.definition: Relating to a postzygapophysis, the back-facing articular process on a vertebra.
examples: Postzygapophyseal joints connect adjacent vertebrae.
They measured postzygapophyseal angles in the spine.
Postzygapophyseal fractures can cause instability.

senses.definition: Relating to a postzygapophysis; another spelling of postzygapophyseal.
examples: Postzygapophysial joints help guide spinal movement.
The scan showed damage to the postzygapophysial surface.
Postzygapophysial changes were noted in older patients.

senses.definition: A back-facing bony process on a vertebra that forms a joint with the vertebra behind it.
examples: Each vertebra has a postzygapophysis that connects to the next bone.
Injury to the postzygapophysis can limit movement.
They studied the shape of the postzygapophysis in different species.

senses.definition: A small bunch of flowers, often given as a gift or carried for decoration.
examples: She carried a small posy of wildflowers.
He bought a posy for his grandmother.
Each guest received a tiny posy at the wedding.

senses.definition: A deep round container, usually used for cooking food or holding liquids.
examples: She stirred the soup in a large pot.
He put the pasta in the pot of boiling water.
The pot was too heavy to lift.

senses.definition: A container used for growing a plant.
examples: The flowers look lovely in that clay pot.
She moved the plant to a bigger pot.
Water the pot twice a week.

senses.definition: A sum of money that players compete to win in a card game, lottery, or competition.
examples: He won the whole pot in the last round.
The prize pot has reached a million dollars.
They each put ten dollars into the pot.

senses.definition: Informal word for marijuana.
examples: He was arrested for smoking pot in the park.
Pot is illegal in some places but legal in others.
They discussed the health effects of pot.

senses.definition: To put a plant into a pot with soil so it can grow.
examples: We need to pot these seedlings today.
She carefully potted the new rose bush.
They pot the young plants in spring.

senses.definition: The qualities of different possible options or opportunities, especially when considering what might be done.
examples: She considered the various potabilities before choosing a career path.
The book explores the potabilities of human imagination.
They discussed the political potabilities at length.

senses.definition: The quality of water being safe and pleasant to drink.
examples: They tested the potability of the local water supply.
The river’s potability has decreased due to pollution.
Potability standards are set by health authorities.

senses.definition: Safe and good enough to drink.
examples: The tap water here is potable.
They had to bring in potable water by truck.
Not all river water is potable without treatment.

senses.definition: A drink, especially an alcoholic one.
examples: They offered a range of potables at the reception.
The bar serves local potables and snacks.
He enjoyed sampling the region’s potables.

senses.definition: The state of being safe and pleasant to drink.
examples: They assessed the potableness of the spring water.
Filters can improve the potableness of tap water.
Pollution threatened the potableness of the river.

senses.definition: Different degrees or kinds of being safe and pleasant to drink.
examples: The study compared the potablenesses of several water sources.
Seasonal changes affect the potablenesses of local wells.
They measured the potablenesses using standard tests.
